item.title: Two years in hrt. I'm much happier now, even if the after picture doesn't show it, lol
item.description: I know, no selfies, but maybe a pride month exception? The picture on the left is about -5 years. It's legit one of the most recent pictures of me before my transition. The right picture was taken just before my two year transiversary.
item.dc:creator: ArchonIlladrya

item.title: Growth By Reduction
item.description: My partner inherited her extra large breasts from her mom who had horrible back problems leading to all kinds of issues such as addiction and abuse throughout her life. I won't go into it here, but it traumatized everyone around her for as long as my partner can remember and she's no longer involved with our family. My love was worried that the same would happen to her and our family and of course didn't want to keep that cycle going. So we planned and we saved. And we did our research and she picked a doctor. And last week, she got her transformation. It was painful and is exhausting in recovery but she's tough as nails. I'm trying my best to help her recover and work back up to being 100% again. My biggest worry is that she'll push herself too hard and hurt herself. But I'm trusting her and her body. The first thing she said to me in the recovery room was "hi", but the thing that will always stick with me is: "It feels like I finally got rid of my mom."
item.dc:creator: cptbahab

item.title: The best reaction to being recognized
item.dc:creator: cuckimtran66331415

item.title: From not knowing if he'd survive, on life support, major surgery and complications, constant hospital visits, blood transfusions, multiple bowel blockages (that still happen and have us living in fear) my superhero has made it to 6!! He's been through so
item.description: Honestly don't know why I'm posting this, but the relief, the emotions of seeing him celebrating his 6th birthday has me all over the place!! We never thought we'd get here! He has so much going on in his little life, been through so much, I honestly don't know how he's survived and managed to stay so happy and kind! He has speech apraxia, SPD, dyspraxia, risk of bowel blockages everyday, emergency hospital visits due to a couple of blockages etc. He could be so mad at the world, but he just isn't! He's a fucking rockstar!
item.dc:creator: DumpingSh1tandBeingKind2

item.title: EV sales slipping? That's incorrect.
item.description: We bought a Chevy Equinox EV. Already have a Chevy Bolt, and it's our second one at that. When 'the facts' say the EVs only have 7% of the market, and Hybrids account for only 9% of the new vehicle market...I have to wonder about graph being skewed. All I know, there were 12 new Blazer and Equinox EVs delivered to the local dealership in May, and we got the last one. More coming, the dealership said that there are back orders for certain trim packages. Facts about the Tesla brand: 1. Tesla's brand is poisonous among educated, successful progressives thanks to Elmo. This is the primary market for EVs. 2. Tesla's designs are stale, the fit and finish are horrible, and they have fires in a way that other EV makers do not. 3. When your flagship new product makes people point and laugh, it is not a good thing. That cyber 'truck' is an overpriced joke. 4. Tesla's are no longer prestigious. The Model 3 is the new Nissan Sentra. Yikes #EVs
item.dc:creator: doxydad1

item.title: Exhibit of the day!
item.description: A colossal amethyst geode! At ~13 ft (4 m) tall & 9,000Ibs (4,082 kg), it's as heavy as 3 compact cars. Formed 135 million years ago, it started as colorless quartz-becoming purple after millennia of natural radiation, heat, & trace contaminants.
item.dc:creator: DeadStar87

item.title: Your Wednesday Wholesome dump of smiles and hugs

1
2. Screw lying politicians, we need more of this guy : )
3. Awwww
4. Pet the good boy
5
6. Pinnacle of dad being funny
7. Respect for your Ebbie
8. Sighs...
9. Just a dog and his ballon
10. That last scoop is epic
11. The "Don't tell mom look"
12. The destructive energy is so adorable
13. Dog empathizing with its owner
14. The day cat and roomba ended their friendship
15. Hello friendos, for the past year and a half, I’ve been working on a game that’s very close to my heart. "Do Not Press The Button (To Delete The Multiverse)" is a quirky adventure crafted with love and passion, with my brother by my side, helping every step of the way. As indie developers, we rely on your support to bring our game to life. By adding it to your wishlist, you’re helping us reach more players and make a big impact in the gaming community. https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse/ We can’t do this without you! Thank you for supporting our dream and helping us share our wild adventure with the world!
item.dc:creator: TheseusGames

item.title: So this is how they teach history in Mississippi
item.description:
Ok, wife and I were down in Mississippi and decided to go and visit the Mississippi Agracultural Museum in Canton MS. Wife loved it as a kid, and was a pretty nice place last time I went maybe 20 years ago. Things have.... changed....
The utter insanity of some of the displays boggled my mind. "The task system allowed specific assignments for individual slaves and created incentives for those who worked rapidly and productively". Yeah.... What an amazing time to be a slave! But hey, the living conditions of slaves improved substantially in the 1840's and wow, you could become a highly skilled workman if you worked at it. AS A F$*&&*$#@(*&($#@ING SLAVE!!!!! Other panels talk about how great slavery was until the "event" in the 1860's that resulted in Black people having terrible lives. Yeah, that "Civil war" thing was just an "economic event" and man it sure seemed to be a negative thing for black people. WHAT THE BLOODY HELL????? This is the kind of stuff that the State Government of Mississippi teaches in their "educational institutions". I guess they show "Song of the South" in third grade to kids to tell them how great things really were and could be if people would JUST GO BACK TO BEING SLAVES! Ahem. Those who fail to learn from history wind up repeating it. I have heard this said, but never had it whacked so hard into my face as when I visited this "Museum". Hint, ask the people working there what tribes the (very White looking) Native Americans were. You won't get an answer because at this museum they have no clue. But boy all the White Guys who came over sure have names and pictures.... I'll go back to my self imposed Imgur exile now, but this blew my mind so much I had to come and post about it. Have a great day....
item.dc:creator: lightfoot2

item.title: LGBTQ and trans coming out
item.description:
This selfie is important because it signifies me finally coming to terms with being transgender.
I took another one and made it black and white. I don't hate how my makeup turned out for once. At least I look human and functioning.
Again I look kind of nice and human. I just need to gather the courage to tell my parents. Even though they support me, I'm nervous AF because they're from a different time, a time where they weren't educated on gender and sexuality.
I'm trans femme, but also like they/them. Never he/him or his please. That was the me faking it, the me not even realising I was an egg until I learned from the internet. Have the following memes to offset this. Love to all LGBTQIA2+ people for pride, all year and every year. You're important and deserve to be here. You deserver to enjoy life. FO to anyone who wants top do us harm x. You can hate me, but you'll never break me! I'm one bad B and I'll fight for LGBTQ rights. L esbian G ay B isexual T ransgender Q ueer I ntersex A sexual/Agender 2 spirit + all others not covered like gender fluid, demi, pansexual, a sexual, unsure, poly, flexible.
item.dc:creator: SolidSyco

item.title: Are you sad? Or heartbroken?
item.description: Well sauna is answer for all your problems. We finnish people are sad. But with sauna. We are now sad and steaming hot. Like sad hot dogs. Edit: Front page! Jumalajita! Nyt poijjat jal likat! Torille! Mää tarjoon! Steamed and sad hot dogs are heading to Torille! After that! More sauna!

item.title: Being an Ally
item.description: Attended the Pride March in Newcastle, Washington because the rightwing asshole mayor refused to fly the Pride flag at city hall, comparing it to divisive imagery like the Hamas flag. Great time. We won, and there were a few zealot assholes there to cause trouble, but they wilt away when faced with this 6'4", 300 lbs Norse-Gaelic ally. #pridemonth #prideally

item.title: Health insurance won't cover your medication? Here is a couple tips!
item.description: As we (Americans) all know, American health care sucks the big rotten Richard. If you have illnesses such as IBD, IBS, or other medical conditions that insurance won't cover because it's considered "experimental" or just they will flat out not cover it after several prior authorizations have been sent between your MD's office and them, then you have a couple options 1) check out some of the Canadian pharmacies such as https://www.drugmart.com/ and they may be able to fill your medication at half the out of pocket price that you would have to pay here in the states. Some medications that are usually $1500-$2000 and up for things like a 90 day supply could be some times less than or up to a $100. You would still have to pay out of pocket, but it's an option for you. 2) Check out GoodRx. Some places like Albertson's, Costco, Safeway ect can get those super spendy meds down to $20 for a 30 day supply. But there is a caviat, it won't work if you have Medicare (which sucks big time but refer to tip number 1. 3) If you have commercial insurance such as through your work and INSURANCE WILL STILL NOT PAY, check out the drug company web site and you may be able to get a prescription copay card, that you would only have to renew yearly. For example, I take Amiovig once a month for migraines, but with my insurance I would have to pay $1500 out of pocket. But with the copay card, it's only $5 a month. 4) FINALLY Huge, huge tip! If a Canadian pharmacy is too expensive, if GoodRx isn't an option, and you don't have commercial insurance, ask your doctor's office about prescription assistance programs. If you have medicare, medicaide, or no insurance at all, then this could be a fantastic option. These programs are put into place in hospitals and clinics to help you get your medication with a 90 day supply and you won't have to pay anything. You would just have to fill out the paperwork, the clinic faxes it back to the drug company, and then once approved, they will ship it to either you or your clinic. Keep in mind, this is probably not available every where and won't work for all medications such as pain management meds or cancer treatment medications (however if you are going through cancer treatments check out the American Cancer Society and the Leukemia/Lymphoma Society and they offer assistance as well including paying for travel to your appointments and accommodations while you go through treatment such as an AirBnB). Remember you can advocate for yourself! You have the power as the patient and knowing that these resources are available to you, you can get medications :) Personal story: In 2008 I was in a very deep depression and had zero insurance. I needed antidepressants and was able to get get a 90 day supply of Cymbalta for free :) Good luck! Sorry for a long post
item.dc:creator: memesarethebestever
